title: Black Pepper Kale Soup
description: A smoky and light Ethiopian dish featuring black pepper caramelized in a broth with kale to create a comforting sick-day soup that is kosher-friendly.
instructions: Bring chicken or vegetable broth and water to a boil in a large pot.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add salt, black pepper, turmeric, cumin, garlic, and onion; cook for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add kale leaves to the pot with liquid. Reduce heat to low and simmer for about 10-15 minutes or until kale is tender.
Stir in butternut squash and chopped celery. Simmer another 10 minutes.
Using a potato masher or immersion blender, puree the soup until smooth.
Return soup to pot, cover and let it warm through before serving.
